ogTitle: Friendly Beaches vs Gagebrook: Climate Safety

heading: Friendly Beaches vs Gagebrook — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Friendly Beaches, Tasmania and Gagebrook, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Friendly Beaches has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Gagebrook (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Friendly Beaches or Gagebrook safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Friendly Beaches has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Gagebrook scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Friendly Beaches scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Gagebrook scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Friendly Beaches or Gagebrook?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Friendly Beaches has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.82% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
